title: Apply with statement pattern for database connections across all scripts
description: Several scripts open database connections but don't use context managers (with statements), leading to potential resource leaks. Apply the pattern used in armor.py (trait_db_pass and armor_group_pass) to all other database connection usage.
design: Survey all scripts that use get_db_connection() or create_db() and update them to use 'with' statements for proper resource management. Includes loader scripts (pf2_*_load) and parser modules.

title: Create strength requirement object with legacy and remastered values
description: Armor has strength requirements that differ between legacy (stat number like 18) and remastered (modifier like +4). Create object structure with type stat_block_section, subtype stat_requirement, noting it's strength, with both legacy and remastered values calculated using standard stat-to-modifier conversion. This is for armor output, not database storage.
notes:
COMPLETED: Implemented strength requirement object with legacy and remastered values.

IMPLEMENTATION DETAILS:
- Created _normalize_strength() function in armor.py (lines 478-519)
- Handles both legacy and remastered formats:
  * Legacy format: stat value (e.g., "16") → calculates modifier
  * Remastered format: modifier (e.g., "+3") → calculates stat value
- Detects format by checking for leading '+' sign
- Conversion formulas:
  * Stat to modifier: (stat - 10) // 2
  * Modifier to stat: (modifier * 2) + 10
- Creates structured object with:
  * type: stat_block_section
  * subtype: stat_requirement  
  * stat: strength
  * legacy_value: stat value (10-18)
  * remastered_value: modifier (0-4)
- Added stat_requirement definition to armor.schema.json (lines 73-84)
- Updated armor_stat_block to reference stat_requirement (line 96)

TESTING RESULTS:
- Parsed all 55 armor HTML files successfully
- Generated 51 unique armor JSON files (some sources have same armors)
- 45 armor pieces with strength requirements (all validated correctly)
- 6 armor pieces without strength (unarmored, explorer's clothing, gi, scroll robes)
- All conversions verified:
  * STR 10 / +0 ✓
  * STR 12 / +1 ✓
  * STR 14 / +2 ✓
  * STR 16 / +3 ✓
  * STR 18 / +4 ✓
- Both legacy and remastered versions produce identical strength objects
- Schema validation passes for armor with and without strength requirements
close_reason: Implemented strength requirement object with both legacy (stat value) and remastered (modifier) values. Schema validated successfully.

title: Consolidate duplicate loader scripts into generic script
description: pf2_armor_group_load and pf2_weapon_group_load are nearly identical scripts with different table names. This duplication increases maintenance overhead. Create a single generic loader script that accepts the group type as a command-line argument.
design: Create a generic pf2_item_group_load script that takes group type ('armor_groups', 'weapon_groups') as an argument and dynamically loads the appropriate SQL module. This eliminates duplication and makes it easier to add new group types. Consider whether this pattern applies to other loader scripts as well.

title: Consolidate duplicate parse scripts into generic script
description: pf2_armor_group_parse and pf2_weapon_group_parse are nearly identical scripts that only differ in the subtype they set. They could be merged into a single script that accepts the group type as a command-line argument.
design: Create a generic pf2_item_group_parse script that accepts the item group type ('armor_group' or 'weapon_group') as a command-line argument to determine the subtype and final directory name for exec_main. This reduces code duplication and makes it easier to add new group types. Consider whether this pattern applies to other parse scripts as well.

title: Modernize shell scripts with best practices
description: Shell scripts (pf2_run_*.sh) have several areas for improvement flagged in PR review: using input redirection instead of 'cat | while read', using 'read -r' to prevent backslash interpretation, using 'break' instead of 'exit' in loops to avoid subshell issues, and using $(command) instead of backticks.
design: Apply these improvements consistently to all pf2_run_*.sh scripts: 1) Replace 'cat file | while read i' with 'while read -r i; do ... done < file', 2) Use 'break' instead of 'exit' in while loops, 3) Use $(command) for command substitution instead of backticks. Affects: pf2_run_armor.sh, pf2_run_armor_groups.sh, pf2_run_weapon_groups.sh, and other runner scripts.

title: Archives of Nethys removed ammunition fields from weapons HTML
description: The live website (2e.aonprd.com) appears to have removed Ammunition fields from many weapons in their current code. This is an error on their end. Our local HTML files still contain the correct Ammunition field (e.g., Axe Musket ID 213 has 'Firearm Ammunition (10 rounds)' in the Ranged section). When we do the next HTML update from Archives of Nethys, we need to verify and potentially fix the missing ammunition fields.
design:
Before next HTML update:
1. Document which weapons currently have ammunition fields in our local HTML
2. After HTML update, compare to see which ammunition fields were removed
3. If ammunition fields are missing in new HTML, consider:
   - Contacting Archives of Nethys to report the bug
   - Manually re-adding ammunition fields to the HTML
   - Adding ammunition data from another source (like official PDFs)

Location evidence:
- Local HTML: pfsrd2-web/2e.aonprd.com/Weapons/Weapons.aspx.ID_213.html (line ~4414)
- Parser extracts: ammunition appears in ranged mode for combination weapons
- Schema: ammunition field exists in weapon_mode definition
acceptance_criteria:
- [ ] Documented list of weapons with ammunition in current HTML
- [ ] Verified ammunition fields after HTML update
- [ ] Decision made on how to handle missing fields (if any)
notes:
## Ammunition Field HTML Documentation Analyzed the pfsrd2-data git diff and documented HTML structure for weapons with ammunition fields. ### Pattern Identified All ammunition fields follow the same structure: ``` Ammunition [Ammunition Type] ``` ### Location in HTML 1. **Simple Ranged Weapons** (Crossbow, Longbow, Sling): - Ammunition appears AFTER Group field - At weapon level (not in mode section) - Pattern: Price → Damage → Range → Reload → Type → Category → Group → Ammunition 2. **Firearms** (Flintlock Pistol): - Same position as simple ranged - After Group field at weapon level 3. **Combination Weapons** (Axe Musket): - Ammunition appears in Ranged mode section - After Group field within the Ranged h2 section - Pattern: Melee section → Ranged section (Damage → Range → Reload → Group → Ammunition) ### Examples from Local HTML **Crossbow (ID 67)** - Line 4368: ``` Group Bow
Ammunition Bolts ``` **Longbow (ID 76)** - Line 4417: ``` Ammunition Arrows ``` **Sling (ID 72)** - Line 4371: ``` Ammunition Sling Bullets ``` **Flintlock Pistol (ID 192)** - Line 4387: ``` Ammunition Firearm Ammunition (10 rounds) ``` **Axe Musket (ID 213)** - Line 4414 (in Ranged section): ``` Group Firearm
Ammunition Firearm Ammunition (10 rounds) ``` **Taw Launcher (ID 342)** - Line 4403: ``` Ammunition Wooden Taws ``` **Repeating Hand Crossbow (ID 177)** - Line 4384: ``` Ammunition Repeating Hand Crossbow Magazine ``` ### Affected Weapons Count From git diff analysis: **79 weapons** have ammunition fields in local HTML, including: - All bows (longbow, shortbow, composite bows) - All crossbows (crossbow, hand crossbow, heavy crossbow, repeating variants) - All firearms (pistols, muskets, etc.) - Slings and specialized launchers - Combination weapons with ranged modes ### Verification Status ✅ Local HTML files (/pfsrd2-web/) contain ammunition fields ❌ Live website (2e.aonprd.com) missing these fields (reported error on their end) ✅ Parser correctly extracts ammunition when present in HTML

title: Refactor SQL fetch functions to return data instead of cursor pattern
description: Currently all SQL fetch_* functions execute queries but leave results in cursor (caller uses curs.fetchone()). This pattern is inconsistent with modern Python conventions and was flagged in PR review. Survey all pfsrd2/sql/*.py modules and update fetch functions to return data directly.
design: Update all fetch_* functions in SQL modules to return curs.fetchone() or curs.fetchall() as appropriate. Update all callers to use returned value instead of calling curs.fetchone() separately. Affects: traits.py, armor_groups.py, weapon_groups.py, and other SQL modules.

title: Migrate from deprecated optparse to argparse
description: The optparse module has been deprecated since Python 3.2. All loader scripts (pf2_*_load) currently use optparse and should migrate to the modern argparse module for better long-term support and features.
design: Replace optparse.OptionParser with argparse.ArgumentParser in all loader scripts. Update argument parsing from '(options, args) = parser.parse_args()' to 'options = parser.parse_args()' for scripts without positional arguments. Affects: pf2_armor_group_load, pf2_weapon_group_load, and other loader scripts using optparse.

title: Consolidate duplicate runner scripts into generic script
description: pf2_run_armor.sh, pf2_run_armor_groups.sh, and pf2_run_weapon_groups.sh share large amounts of boilerplate code. This logic could be consolidated into a single generic runner script to improve maintainability.
design: Create a generic runner script that takes the content type as a parameter and dynamically sets log file names, parser script, and source directory. For example: 'pf2_run.sh weapon_group' would set PF2_WEB_DIR/WeaponGroups, errors.pf2.weapon_group.log, and call pf2_weapon_group_parse. Consider whether this pattern applies to other runner scripts beyond just groups.

title: Abstract duplicate SQL modules into generic item_groups pattern
description: pfsrd2/sql/armor_groups.py and pfsrd2/sql/weapon_groups.py are near-identical with only table/column names changed. This duplication makes schema changes difficult and error-prone. Create a generic pattern to eliminate this duplication.
design: Consider creating a factory function or base class that generates SQL helper functions (create_table, create_index, truncate, insert, fetch) based on table name and configuration. This would centralize logic for simple lookup tables. May also apply to other SQL modules with similar patterns. Investigate whether a generic pfsrd2/sql/item_groups.py module makes sense or if a factory pattern is better.

title: Use multiline string literals for SQL queries
description: SQL queries in pfsrd2/sql modules use '\n'.join([...]) pattern which is harder to read and edit than multiline string literals (triple quotes). Converting to triple-quoted strings would improve readability and maintainability.
design: Replace all SQL query construction using '\n'.join([list]) with triple-quoted multiline strings. For example: sql = '''CREATE TABLE armor_groups (...) '''. Affects all pfsrd2/sql/*.py modules including armor_groups.py, weapon_groups.py, traits.py, etc.

title: Implement armor groups with linking support
description: Armor currently has group field as string, but it should link to full ArmorGroup objects. Need to create parser for ArmorGroups from pfsrd-web data, add to database, integrate into pf2_run_deps.sh, and update armor parser to swap group strings with full objects (similar to how traits work).

title: Create ArmorGroups parser for pfsrd-web data
description: Create parser to read ArmorGroup data from pfsrd-web. Use monster_abilities parser as reference pattern. Need to investigate ArmorGroup structure in pfsrd-web/ArmorGroup to determine fields.

title: Add armor_groups table to database schema
description: Add armor_groups table to database schema. Structure will be determined after investigating ArmorGroup data format. Should follow similar pattern to traits table.

title: Integrate ArmorGroups into pf2_run_deps.sh
description: Add ArmorGroups parsing step to pf2_run_deps.sh dependency chain. ArmorGroups must be parsed before armor so that armor parser can reference the full objects.

title: Update armor parser to swap group strings with full ArmorGroup objects
description: Modify armor parser to query database for ArmorGroup objects and replace group string references with full objects. Follow the pattern used for traits swapping.
notes:
COMPLETED: Implemented armor_group_pass to enrich armor groups with full database data.

IMPLEMENTATION DETAILS:
- Added import for fetch_armor_group_by_name from pfsrd2.sql.armor_groups (line 33)
- Created armor_group_pass() function in armor.py (lines 766-803)
  * Walks structure to find objects with subtype='armor_group'
  * Queries database by armor group name
  * Replaces minimal armor group object with full database version
  * Removes aonid, license, and schema_version fields (embedded objects don't need these)
  * Pattern follows trait_db_pass() implementation
- Added armor_group_pass() call in parse_armor() pipeline (line 60, after trait_db_pass)
- Updated armor_group definition in armor.schema.json (lines 62-81)
  * Changed from minimal structure (type: stat_block_section, subtype: armor_group) 
  * To enriched structure (type: armor_group with text, sources, edition, game-id, etc.)
  * Required fields: type, name, game-id, game-obj, text, sources, edition
  * Optional fields: sections, links

BEFORE ENRICHMENT (extracted from HTML):
{
  "type": "stat_block_section",
  "subtype": "armor_group",
  "name": "Plate",
  "link": {"type": "link", "name": "Plate", ...}
}

AFTER ENRICHMENT (from database):
{
  "type": "armor_group",
  "name": "Plate",
  "text": "The sturdy plate provides no purchase for a cutting edge. You gain resistance to slashing damage...",
  "sources": [...],
  "edition": "remastered",
  "game-id": "572e11ba7052dd62c4872109f22f3d53",
  "game-obj": "ArmorGroups"
}

TESTING RESULTS:
- Parsed all 55 armor HTML files successfully
- Generated 51 unique armor JSON files
- 48 armor pieces with enriched armor groups ✓
- 3 armor pieces without groups (Explorer's Clothing, Unarmored, etc.) ✓
- All enriched groups have:
  * type: "armor_group" ✓
  * text field with special property description ✓
  * sources array ✓
  * edition (legacy/remastered) ✓
  * game-id ✓
- Schema validation passes for all files ✓

Examples verified:
- Plate group: "The sturdy plate provides no purchase..." ✓
- Chain group: "The armor is so flexible it can bend..." ✓
- Composite group: "The numerous overlapping pieces..." ✓
- Leather group: enriched with full data ✓
close_reason: Successfully implemented armor_group_pass to enrich armor groups with full database data. All 48 armor pieces with groups now have enriched data including text descriptions, sources, and edition information. Pattern follows trait_db_pass implementation.
